back:
Browser-based cloud IDE with a built-in terminal that has direct AWS CLI access; underlying compute typically runs on an EC2 instance you own.

Mostly a developer-convenience/collaboration tool — low weight on the SAP-C02 exam beyond simple recognition.

Managed artifact repository for language-native package managers (npm, Maven, pip, NuGet, etc.), letting teams host private packages and proxy public repositories (e.g. npmjs, PyPI) through a single controlled endpoint.

Fully managed build service: compiles source, runs tests, and produces deployable artifacts, scaling build capacity automatically and billing per build-minute.

It's the 'build' stage that plugs into CodePipeline, and a frequent exam pattern is running CodeBuild inside a VPC to reach private resources (e.g. an RDS instance) during integration tests.

Managed private Git repository hosting on AWS, integrated with IAM for access control.

Note: AWS has been deprioritizing CodeCommit for new customers in favor of third-party Git providers (GitHub, GitLab, Bitbucket) integrated via CodeStar Connections/CodePipeline — exam questions may still reference it, but recognize third-party Git integration as the more current pattern.

Automates code deployments to EC2, on-premises servers, Lambda, or ECS, supporting in-place and blue/green deployment strategies with automatic rollback on failed health checks/alarms.

Exam-critical: CodeDeploy is specifically the 'deploy' stage — for Lambda/ECS it enables canary and linear traffic-shifting deployments, a common exam scenario for minimizing blast radius of a bad release.

Orchestrates the end-to-end CI/CD release process (source → build → test → deploy) by chaining other services (CodeCommit/GitHub, CodeBuild, CodeDeploy, CloudFormation, etc.) into automated stages triggered on every code change.

Think of it as the 'glue'/orchestrator connecting the individual Code* services and third-party tools into one pipeline.

Distributed tracing service that visualizes request paths across microservices (a 'service map'), pinpointing latency bottlenecks and errors across Lambda, API Gateway, ECS, EC2, etc. Exam trigger: 'identify which specific service in a distributed/microservices call chain is causing latency or errors' — X-Ray traces individual requests end-to-end, which CloudWatch metrics alone can't do.

Toolchain/framework for building and hosting full-stack web/mobile apps quickly, bundling auth (via Cognito), API (via AppSync/API Gateway), storage (S3), and CI/CD-based hosting into a guided developer workflow.

Mostly a frontend/mobile developer productivity tool — light exam weight beyond recognizing what it bundles.

Runs automated tests for web/mobile apps against real physical devices and desktop browsers in the cloud, without provisioning test infrastructure yourself.

Niche, low-weight exam topic — recognize it as the answer to 'test my app across many real mobile devices/browsers.'

ML-powered code review (CodeGuru Reviewer) and application performance profiling (CodeGuru Profiler) — automatically flags bugs, security issues, and inefficient code patterns during development/CI, and identifies CPU/latency hotspots in running applications.

Platform-engineering tool letting a central team define standardized infrastructure/deployment templates for containerized and serverless apps, which developers then self-service deploy from — aimed at large orgs wanting to enforce consistent architecture patterns without developers writing their own IaC.

front: ECR vs. CodeArtifact — what's the difference?
back:
ECR stores container images.

CodeArtifact stores language/library packages (npm, Maven, pip, etc.) — not container images.
